title: Commensuration torques in double-moiré twisted trilayer hexagonal boron nitride and graphene heterostructures
authors: Youngju Park, Nicolas Leconte, Prathap Kumar Jharapla, Md Shaifullah, E. H. Hwang, Jeil Jung
abstract: We study commensuration-driven torques and angle locking in double-moiré trilayer hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) and graphene heterostructures using large-scale atomistic relaxations. In twisted trilayer hBN (t3BN) homostructures, double-moiré commensuration ($θ_{12} = -θ_{23}$) give rise to local energy minima accompanied by torque sign reversals, signaling a restoring tendency toward the commensurate configuration. The corresponding binding energies are $\sim$0.2-0.3 meV/atom, originating from enhanced overlap of low-energy stacking domains, although the system is globally stable at zero twist. In contrast, in graphene/hBN heterolayers systems the global energy minimum can coincide with the double-moiré commensuration angle, particularly near $\sim$0.6$^{\circ}$, reflecting competition between lattice mismatch and interfacial relaxation. Incommensurate atomic structures have reduced stabilization due to suppressed overlap of low-energy stacking and have enhanced superlubricity due to spatial averaging of interfacial energies. These results establish double-moiré commensuration as a general, system-dependent mechanism for twist-angle stabilization, whose angular stability is characterized by the torque magnitude and binding energy. Coulomb electrostatic interactions further enhance the stabilization energy without changing the underlying physics.
summary: This paper explores how the alignment of two moiré interfaces in trilayer 2D heterostructures leads to 'angle-locking' via commensuration torques. It demonstrates that the overlap of favorable stacking regions stabilizes specific twist angles, providing a mechanism for controlling the structural and mechanical properties of moiré superlattices.
main_problem: Investigating the energetic origins and stability of commensuration-driven torques and angle-locking in double-moiré trilayer heterostructures.
main_result: The study identifies that double-moiré commensuration creates local energy minima and restoring torques due to the enhanced overlap of low-energy stacking domains, with graphene/hBN systems potentially exhibiting global energy minima at specific angles.
method: Large-scale atomistic relaxations using the LAMMPS molecular dynamics package with various interatomic potentials (Tersoff, REBO2, and DRIP force-fields) and electrostatic models.
model_or_system: Trilayer heterostructures including twisted trilayer hBN (t3BN) homostructures and graphene/hBN heterolayers (e.g., G/BN/G, G/BN/BN) with varying twist angles and lattice mismatches.
key_observables: Total energy per atom, torque (angular derivative of energy), binding energy, and stacking configuration maps.
important_parameters: Twist angles (theta_12, theta_32), lattice mismatch, torque constants, and electrostatic interaction types (short-range vs. long-range).
main_assumptions: Use of commensurate approximants to allow for periodic boundary conditions in incommensurate phases.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows energy maps and energy variation vs. twist angle; Figure 2 illustrates local stacking configuration maps; Figure 3 displays sliding-dependent energy maps and binding energy trends for G/BN/G and related systems; Figure 4 shows total energy per atom evolution.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of double-moiré stability, details the atomistic simulation methodology, presents results for t3BN homostructures, extends the analysis to graphene/hBN heterostructures, and discusses the role of electrostatic interactions and superlubricity.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (color online) Sliding- and twist-angle-dependent total energies per atom (Etot) for t3BN systems. (a) Sliding-dependent energy maps for double-moir´e commensurate structures at θ12 = θ32 = 1.08◦, 1.54◦and 2.00◦for different relative orientations between the hBN layers. When sliding the top layer, the BN/BN/BN, NB/BN/BN, and BN/NB/BN structures are most stable for the AAA, AAC′, and AA′A configurations, respectively. The overline notation on a stacking label denote a configuration in which that local stacking is placed at the rotation center before applying the twist. Among these three, BN/BN/BN with the AAA rotation center has the lowest total energy. (b) Angle-dependent transition...

FIG. 3. (color online) Total energies per atom for G/BN/G, BN/G/NB, and BN/G/BN systems as a function of sliding and top twist angle θ32 with respect to the middle layer. (a) Sliding-dependent energy maps for commensurate G/BN/G structures at θ12 = θ23 = 0.6, 1.28, 1.78◦show that the AAC configuration are the most stable and smaller twist angles yield lower total energies. (b) θ32-dependent transitions from incommensurate to commensurate G/BN/G structures, showing the local energy minima at the commensurate conditions (indicated by vertical lines). The curves to the left and right of each commensurate angle are extrapolated using parabolic fits, and we approximate reference total-energy...

FIG. 4. (color online) Total energies per atom for G/BN/BN and G/BN/NB systems as a function of (a) sliding and (b) the top-layer twist angle θ32 with respect to the middle layer. Each system is considered for two twist-angle combinations with bottom twist angles θ12 = ±1.20◦(type I and type II) and a common top twist angle θ32 = 0.58◦. All four cases exhibit local energy minima at the double-moir´e commensurate condition (indicated by vertical gray lines). For both stacking sequences, type II is energetically more favorable than type I, with the energy differences on the order of 0.1 meV/atom, and G/BN/BN is more stable than G/BN/NB by about 0.3 meV/atom.
